scenario_results.reason: In-scope request produced an evidence-backed equipment handoff summary with named owners, artifacts, and closure conditions.
Missing permit, approved-plan, and laydown prerequisites blocked delivery or staging and produced a dependency-based blocker summary.
Duplicated ownership was surfaced and escalated without inventing a final owner or dropping the dependency.
Inspection and closeout blockers prevented premature completion until operational-test, O&M, and as-built evidence were complete.
Permit approval, code interpretation, and licensed-trade authority were refused and rerouted with a boundary-safe note.
Safety and staging changes blocked the handoff and were escalated to qualified owners instead of being overridden.
Execution work was rejected and routed out of the coordination lane.
Conflicting schedules and dependency owners were preserved as blockers and escalated with authoritative-record expectations.
Missing tenant systems, inventory sources, and owner maps triggered low-confidence escalation instead of confident routing.
Conflict between public guidance and tenant policy was escalated instead of synthesized into an unsupported answer.
Tenant-specific terminology and system names were adapted while preserving the public permit, inspection, safety, and handoff baseline.
Closure confirmation required destination-owner proof, blocker resolution, and required artifact completeness before transfer was considered complete.

notes: Scenario set covers normal execution, missing prerequisites, duplicated ownership, authority refusal, safety escalation, policy conflict, tenant adaptation, and closure verification.

Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all coordination-handoffs-and-routing abilities, and all construction-and-field-services abilities required by the issue contract.
The package remains explicit that runtime is validated as a spec pack but still `specification-based` until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
